home_read.momentum: Came from behind to win late, leaving the impression of a team that could punish opponents and finish strongly.

tactical_shape: Rotated heavily after clinching progression, with several first-choice players rested and fringe/debut players used
Pochettino made second-half use of Christian Pulisic after he missed the Australia match with a calf injury
The US were more vulnerable to disruption when rotating, but still created chances and played with confidence in a more open, rotated lineup
standout_players: Sebastian Berhalter — arguably the standout on the night; assisted Auston Trusty’s opener from a corner and then scored the US’s second
Auston Trusty — opened his international goalscoring account
Matt Turner — surprise start; made some timely sweeper actions but was unable to stop the three shots he faced (4/10)
Joe Scally — struggled to keep up at times, was pulled out of position on Turkey’s second goal, and his crossing lacked danger (5/10)
Mark McKenzie — bypassed too easily for Turkey’s first goal and struggled in long distribution, though he kept the ball moving into midfield (5/10)
fatigue_injuries: Auston Trusty’s rolled left ankle, though he said it had already been treated
Christian Pulisic was coming back from a calf injury and missed the Australia match
The cautioned quartet were rested/suspended from selection for this match to avoid yellow-card risk: Chris Richards, Antonee Robinson, Folarin Balogun and Tyler Adams
away_read.momentum: The defeat did not seem to dent belief; the players emphasized good vibes, lessons learned, and confidence heading into the knockout stage.
